#+TITLE: Long-form personal systems as a reading surface
#+SUBTITLE: Testing the calm measure that should carry several pages
#+AUTHOR: Hubert Behaghel
#+DATE: 2026-05-10
#+LANGUAGE: en
#+LATEX_CLASS: hub-article
#+EXPORT_EYEBROW: Personal systems

#+begin_standfirst
A long personal article needs enough room for an argument to breathe, enough
structure for the reader to stay oriented, and enough restraint that the margin
remains useful rather than decorative.
#+end_standfirst

* Opening the long measure

The first obligation of the long-form article is not to look impressive at a
glance.  It is to keep a reader with the argument after the first screen of
interest has disappeared.  The page should therefore choose a comfortable line,
leave visible room for peripheral structure, and refuse the temptation to turn a
personal essay into a campaign artefact.

This fixture deliberately repeats prose in ordinary paragraphs so the generated
document has to carry sustained text rather than only a title treatment.  The
export path should preserve that sustained text while the class owns the reading
measure, the page geometry, and the reserved margin zone.[fn:measure]

* The quiet margin

The margin is planned as a sparse zone: a place for restrained markers and future
safe surfaces, not a second column competing with the body.  A calm article can
benefit from peripheral wayfinding, but only when the body remains clearly in
charge of the page.

#+begin_quote
The margin should behave like a promise of structure, not like a loud sidebar.
#+end_quote

Several authoring constraints follow from that promise:

- keep the prose in one column;
- reserve space beside the body without filling every page edge;
- use margin treatment only when it clarifies orientation;
- avoid inheriting gallery-white two-column campaign flow.

The body continues with enough paragraphs to force the layout question.  A short
fixture would let default LaTeX geometry pass unnoticed, while this text makes
the eventual PDF inspection meaningful.  The article is still modest, but it is
long enough to ask whether the reading surface is generous and deliberate.

* Evidence as furniture

Tables and captions should remain calm editorial furniture.  They are present
here to prove that ordinary technical material survives the personal class path
without requiring the later code-heavy slice.

#+CAPTION: A compact table that should stay within the article reading measure.
| Signal | Desired behavior |
|--------+------------------|
| Body   | single-column prose |
| Margin | sparse reserved markers |
| Rhythm | comfortable reading measure |

The table is intentionally small because Slice 3 is about geometry and measure,
not a complete table design system.  It still gives the exporter a caption and
structured content that future layout work must not break.

Source examples need the same editorial restraint.  Code should sit at the same
level as the prose while preserving syntax highlighting for code-heavy arguments
that still belong inside the essay.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(defun hub/article-reading-path (slug locale)
  "Return the review path for SLUG in LOCALE."
  (format "%s/%s" locale slug))
#+end_src

The second example proves that repeated source blocks stay on the same quiet
surface.  The class, not per-block Org options, should own the restrained minted
token palette.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(defun hub/article-review-note (title tags)
  "Compose a compact review note for TITLE and TAGS."
  (list :title title :tags tags :reviewed t))
#+end_src

* Later reading section

Later sections should feel like continuations of the same essay, not like a new
layout mode.  The line length, margin reservation, headings, and captions should
cooperate so that a reader can move through several pages without visual churn.

The final paragraphs keep the fixture long enough for smoke testing while
remaining plain Org.  The exact words matter less than the contract they expose:
Hub Article long-form output should remain single-column, reserve a sparse
margin zone, and define class-owned markers for the comfortable measure that the
next implementation slice will provide.

* Sustaining the argument

A later page should not suddenly become denser because the opener has passed.
The same measured rhythm should continue through ordinary explanatory prose,
with each paragraph short enough to invite reading and long enough to reveal
whether the line length is humane.  The reader should feel that the page has a
spine, not that the text has merely been poured into whatever area LaTeX offered
by default.

This section adds repeated but meaningful editorial material so the fixture can
exercise the eventual page geometry across more than an isolated first page.
When Slice 4 arrives, code and richer technical artefacts can add their own
requirements.  Slice 3 only needs prose, structure, and modest supporting
evidence to prove that the reading surface itself is intentional.

The reserved margin remains mostly quiet in this fixture.  That quietness is the
point: a future marker may live there, but no paragraph should depend on the
margin being full.  The body must work as a complete essay if every peripheral
surface is empty, because the margin is a secondary promise rather than the main
argument.

Another paragraph keeps the eventual smoke PDF honest.  Comfortable measure is
not visible in a sentence fragment; it appears when the eye has crossed the same
width many times and still trusts the page.  The personal article class should
make that trust easy by limiting the body width and leaving the surrounding
space calm.

* Closing with restraint

The closing section repeats the anti-goal in plain language.  This document
should never activate the gallery-white two-column flow, figure-star furniture,
or campaign layout switches.  It should remain a personal article: single
column, serif-led, sparse in the margin, and designed for sustained reading.

If the future class implementation satisfies the markers asserted by the test,
this fixture will give reviewers enough exported material to open the PDF and
judge the experience across several pages.  Until then, the failing selector is
a precise reminder that opener typography alone is not yet a long-form article
system.

[fn:measure] Footnote text should export as supporting evidence for the reading
measure rather than as a layout driver.
